all_pages.title: Everything To Know About the New Google AI Landing Page Patent - WebFX
all_pages.description:
Brandon Lazovic noted a recently published patent from Google that outlines how Google could generate real-time landing pages for e-commerce stores based on user context (new vs returning, brand loyal, price sensitive, etc.).

In short, Google would use AI to generate a page that looks like your website but rebuilds the entire structure of a page dynamically, in real time, and places it at the top of the SERP. 

This throws up all kinds of red flags to me.

For anyone familiar with Google's AIMax (PMax) ad product, you'll know Google's AI efforts in this space cause about as many problems as they solve, including but not limited to:

-Black-boxed attribution
-Budget cannibalization
-Brand misalignment/brand safety issues
-Hallucinated offers

According to the ANA Q2 2025 Programmatic Transparency Benchmark, in 2025 lost global media value in programmatic advertising saw a 34% increase from 2024, reaching $26.8B.

A technology like this will skyrocket that number.

This could also create new challenges for Legal, a loss of Customer Lifetime Value (due to degraded, inconsistent brand experience) and a significantly heightened reliance on the Google ecosystem. 

On top of everything, this will decrease organic traffic, driving up the overall costs of doing business online, decreasing margins and just generally taking money from the business and putting it directly into Google's pocket. 

If you had a website dependent on Google Discover traffic, you know the tragedies that can occur when your traffic is bound to a black box Google controls.

https://lnkd.in/g4nSpcfx
 | 29 comments on LinkedIn

all_pages.title: Everything To Know About the New Google AI Landing Page Patent - WebFX
all_pages.description:
Brandon Lazovic noted a recently published patent from Google that outlines how Google could generate real-time landing pages for e-commerce stores based on user context (new vs returning, brand loyal, price sensitive, etc.).

In short, Google would use AI to generate a page that looks like your website but rebuilds the entire structure of a page dynamically, in real time, and places it at the top of the SERP. 

This throws up all kinds of red flags to me.

For anyone familiar with Google's AIMax (PMax) ad product, you'll know Google's AI efforts in this space cause about as many problems as they solve, including but not limited to:

-Black-boxed attribution
-Budget cannibalization
-Brand misalignment/brand safety issues
-Hallucinated offers

According to the ANA Q2 2025 Programmatic Transparency Benchmark, in 2025 lost global media value in programmatic advertising saw a 34% increase from 2024, reaching $26.8B.

A technology like this will skyrocket that number.

This could also create new challenges for Legal, a loss of Customer Lifetime Value (due to degraded, inconsistent brand experience) and a significantly heightened reliance on the Google ecosystem. 

On top of everything, this will decrease organic traffic, driving up the overall costs of doing business online, decreasing margins and just generally taking money from the business and putting it directly into Google's pocket. 

If you had a website dependent on Google Discover traffic, you know the tragedies that can occur when your traffic is bound to a black box Google controls.

https://lnkd.in/g4nSpcfx
 | 29 comments on LinkedIn

all_pages.title: Everything To Know About the New Google AI Landing Page Patent - WebFX
all_pages.description:
Brandon Lazovic noted a recently published patent from Google that outlines how Google could generate real-time landing pages for e-commerce stores based on user context (new vs returning, brand loyal, price sensitive, etc.).

In short, Google would use AI to generate a page that looks like your website but rebuilds the entire structure of a page dynamically, in real time, and places it at the top of the SERP. 

This throws up all kinds of red flags to me.

For anyone familiar with Google's AIMax (PMax) ad product, you'll know Google's AI efforts in this space cause about as many problems as they solve, including but not limited to:

-Black-boxed attribution
-Budget cannibalization
-Brand misalignment/brand safety issues
-Hallucinated offers

According to the ANA Q2 2025 Programmatic Transparency Benchmark, in 2025 lost global media value in programmatic advertising saw a 34% increase from 2024, reaching $26.8B.

A technology like this will skyrocket that number.

This could also create new challenges for Legal, a loss of Customer Lifetime Value (due to degraded, inconsistent brand experience) and a significantly heightened reliance on the Google ecosystem. 

On top of everything, this will decrease organic traffic, driving up the overall costs of doing business online, decreasing margins and just generally taking money from the business and putting it directly into Google's pocket. 

If you had a website dependent on Google Discover traffic, you know the tragedies that can occur when your traffic is bound to a black box Google controls.

https://lnkd.in/g4nSpcfx
 | 29 comments on LinkedIn

all_pages.title: Everything To Know About the New Google AI Landing Page Patent - WebFX
all_pages.description:
Brandon Lazovic noted a recently published patent from Google that outlines how Google could generate real-time landing pages for e-commerce stores based on user context (new vs returning, brand loyal, price sensitive, etc.).

In short, Google would use AI to generate a page that looks like your website but rebuilds the entire structure of a page dynamically, in real time, and places it at the top of the SERP. 

This throws up all kinds of red flags to me.

For anyone familiar with Google's AIMax (PMax) ad product, you'll know Google's AI efforts in this space cause about as many problems as they solve, including but not limited to:

-Black-boxed attribution
-Budget cannibalization
-Brand misalignment/brand safety issues
-Hallucinated offers

According to the ANA Q2 2025 Programmatic Transparency Benchmark, in 2025 lost global media value in programmatic advertising saw a 34% increase from 2024, reaching $26.8B.

A technology like this will skyrocket that number.

This could also create new challenges for Legal, a loss of Customer Lifetime Value (due to degraded, inconsistent brand experience) and a significantly heightened reliance on the Google ecosystem. 

On top of everything, this will decrease organic traffic, driving up the overall costs of doing business online, decreasing margins and just generally taking money from the business and putting it directly into Google's pocket. 

If you had a website dependent on Google Discover traffic, you know the tragedies that can occur when your traffic is bound to a black box Google controls.

https://lnkd.in/g4nSpcfx
 | 29 comments on LinkedIn
